evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure data quality through testing, monitoring, and documentation
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities repeatedly emphasise testing, monitoring, governance and ownership of business-critical data with no velocity language present.
evidence.domain_risk: critical data models that support decision-making across credit, payments, and fraud/AML domains
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role sits in fintech credit, payments, and fraud/AML — explicit financial and compliance-relevant domains.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Uphold analytics engineering practices including code reviews
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named practices (code reviews, semantic layer, orchestration) in responsibilities imply an established but still-growing function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build and maintain core data models using dbt for critical reporting; Contribute to the semantic layer (LookML)
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise tool references (dbt, LookML, Airflow) and domain context give a hiring-manager level of specificity.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Partner with stakeholders, Data Engineers, and Analysts on requirements
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is the data function itself — engineers and analysts consuming the modelling layer.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role executes scoped modelling and quality work in service of others' requirements rather than setting direction.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools or AI-consuming infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Ensure data quality through testing, monitoring, and documentation
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing is framed as an owned responsibility of the role, not just a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: critical data models that support decision-making across credit, payments, and fraud/AML domains
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High loss aversion: fintech credit/payments/fraud domain plus repeated governance and ownership language throughout.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Support reliable data workflows using orchestration tools like Airflow
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Language of maintaining and scaling existing critical models points to fix/scale rather than greenfield build.
evidence.loss_aversion: Comfort in high engineering/governance standard environments — the JD frames the role around protecting business-critical, compliance-adjacent data.
evidence.work_arrangement: Multiple locations in Poland (Remote, Hybrid, or Office options available)
